revision.text:
{{otheruses|徳島市佐古地区にある八幡神社|徳島市伊賀町にある八幡神社|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 椎宮八幡神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]]佐古山町椎宮1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[木花咲耶姫命]] |社格 = |創建 = 寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 椎宮神社 |例祭 = [[10月16日]] |神事 = }}
'''椎宮八幡神社'''（しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に鎮座する。通称「しいのみやさん」と呼ばれている。別名「'''椎宮神社'''」。椎宮八幡神社一帯は「'''椎宮公園'''」となっている。
== 祭神 == * [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[木花咲耶姫命]] * 合祀 - [[猿田彦神]]、大市姫命、榮大神
== 境内社 == * 中嶋神社、稲荷神社、神明社、鎮守社、天神社、疱瘡神社
== 概要・歴史 == [[名東郡]][[加茂地区 (徳島市)|矢三村]]の古八幡宮より寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]）に当地へ遷座したと伝わる。[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られ、約3,000本のツツジが植えられている。神社の東側斜面は全面をツツジで覆われており、毎年5月に満開を迎える。
境内には阿波の民話「[[阿波の狸合戦]]」に登場する狸を祀った祠がある他、[[眉山湧水群]]のひとつ青龍水が湧き出し、神社裏には雲龍水も湧き出している。これら湧水群は[[とくしま水紀行50選]]に選定されている。
== 祭事 == * [[10月16日]] - 例大祭
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「佐古七番町」下車、徒歩約7分。
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://kamnavi.jp/en/awa/sii8man.htm 椎宮八幡神社]（個人サイト） * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html 椎宮神社のつつじ]（阿波ナビ）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しいのみやはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:八幡神社]] [[Category:眉山]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{otheruses|徳島市佐古地区にある八幡神社|徳島市伊賀町にある八幡神社|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 椎宮八幡神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]]佐古山町椎宮1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[木花咲耶姫命]] |社格 = |創建 = 寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 椎宮神社 |例祭 = [[10月16日]] |神事 = }}
'''椎宮八幡神社'''（しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に鎮座する。通称「しいのみやさん」と呼ばれている。別名「'''椎宮神社'''」。椎宮八幡神社一帯は「'''椎宮公園'''」となっている。
== 祭神 == * [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[木花咲耶姫命]] * 合祀 - [[猿田彦神]]、大市姫命、榮大神
== 境内社 == * 中嶋神社、稲荷神社、神明社、鎮守社、天神社、疱瘡神社
== 概要・歴史 == [[名東郡]][[加茂地区 (徳島市)|矢三村]]の古八幡宮より寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]）に当地へ遷座したと伝わる。[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られ、約3,000本のツツジが植えられている。神社の東側斜面は全面をツツジで覆われており、毎年5月に満開を迎える。
境内には阿波の民話「[[阿波の狸合戦]]」に登場する狸を祀った祠がある他、[[眉山湧水群]]のひとつ青龍水が湧き出し、神社裏には雲龍水も湧き出している。これら湧水群は[[とくしま水紀行50選]]に選定されている。
== 祭事 == * [[10月16日]] - 例大祭
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「佐古七番町」下車、徒歩約7分。
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://kamnavi.jp/en/awa/sii8man.htm 椎宮八幡神社]（個人サイト） * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html 椎宮神社のつつじ]（阿波ナビ）

== 概要・歴史 == [[名東郡]][[加茂地区 (徳島市)|矢三村]]の古八幡宮より寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]）に当地へ遷座したと伝わる。[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られ、約3,000本のツツジが植えられている。神社の東側斜面は全面をツツジで覆われており、毎年5月に満開を迎える。
境内には阿波の民話「[[阿波の狸合戦]]」に登場する狸を祀った祠がある他、[[眉山湧水群]]のひとつ青龍水が湧き出し、神社裏には雲龍水も湧き出している。これら湧水群は[[とくしま水紀行50選]]に選定されている。神社周辺には[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]や[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。

{{otheruses|徳島市佐古地区にある八幡神社|徳島市伊賀町にある八幡神社|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 椎宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]]佐古山町椎宮1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]] |社格 = |創建 = 寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 椎宮神社 |例祭 = [[10月16日]] |神事 = }}
'''椎宮八幡神社'''（しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に鎮座する。通称「しいのみやさん」と呼ばれている。別名「'''椎宮神社'''」。椎宮八幡神社一帯は「'''椎宮公園'''」となっている。
== 祭神 == * [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]] * 合祀 - [[猿田彦神]]、大市姫命、榮大神
== 境内社 == * 中嶋神社、稲荷神社、神明社、鎮守社、天神社、疱瘡神社
== 概要・歴史 == [[名東郡]][[加茂地区 (徳島市)|矢三村]]の古八幡宮（現・徳島市矢三町高見229）より寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]）に当地へ遷座したと伝わる。古八幡宮には樹齢千年の楠木がある。当神社は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られ、約3,000本のツツジが植えられている。神社の東側斜面は全面をツツジで覆われており、毎年5月に満開を迎える。
境内には[[阿波国|阿波]]の民話「[[阿波狸合戦]]」に登場する狸を祀った祠がある他、[[眉山湧水群]]のひとつ青龍水が湧き出し、神社裏には雲龍水も湧き出している。これら湧水群は[[とくしま水紀行50選]]に選定されている。神社周辺には[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]や[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
== 祭事 == * [[10月16日]] - 例大祭
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古七番町]]」下車、徒歩約7分。

'''椎宮八幡神社'''（しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に鎮座する。通称「しいのみやさん」と呼ばれている。別名「'''椎宮神社'''」。椎宮八幡神社一帯は「'''椎宮公園'''」となっている。[[とくしま市民遺産]]選定。
== 祭神 == * [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]] * 合祀 - [[猿田彦神]]、[[神大市比売|大市姫命]]、榮大神
== 境内社 == * 中嶋神社、稲荷神社、神明社、鎮守社、天神社、疱瘡神社
== 概要・歴史 == [[名東郡]]矢三村の古八幡宮（現・徳島市矢三町高見229）より寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]）に当地へ遷座したと伝わる。古八幡宮には樹齢千年の楠木がある。当神社は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られ、約3,000本のツツジが植えられている。神社の東側斜面は全面をツツジで覆われており、毎年5月に満開を迎える。
境内には[[阿波国|阿波]]の民話「[[阿波狸合戦]]」に登場する狸を祀った祠がある他、[[眉山湧水群]]のひとつ青龍水が湧き出し、神社裏には雲龍水も湧き出している。これら湧水群は[[とくしま水紀行50選]]に選定されている。神社周辺には[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]や[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
== 祭事 == * [[10月16日]] - 例大祭
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古七番町]]」下車、徒歩約7分。
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html 椎宮神社のつつじ]（阿波ナビ）

== 歴史 == [[名東郡]]矢三村の古八幡宮（現・徳島市南矢三町１丁目10-26、{{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|位置|}}）より寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]）に当地へ遷座したと伝わる。古八幡宮には樹齢千年の楠木がある。当神社は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られ、約3,000本のツツジが植えられている。神社の東側斜面は全面をツツジで覆われており、毎年5月に満開を迎える。
境内には[[阿波国|阿波]]の民話「[[阿波狸合戦]]」に登場する狸を祀った祠がある他、[[眉山湧水群]]のひとつ青龍水が湧き出し、神社裏には雲龍水も湧き出している。これら湧水群は[[とくしま水紀行50選]]に選定されている。神社周辺には[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]や[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。

[[ja:%E6%A4%8E%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:redirect]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[category:short pages]] [[category:short pages]][[Category:Shortest 100 pages as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:new pages]] {{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city#Japan Tokushima#Japan |deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: - [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection - [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): - [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – Deity of guidance and pathways - [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce - Ei Daijin (榮大神) – Deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * **Unryūsui (雲龍水)** – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * **Seiryūsui (青龍水)** – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)

[[ja:%E6%A4%8E%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:redirect]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[category:short pages]] [[category:short pages]][[Category:Shortest 100 pages as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:new pages]] {{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city#Japan Tokushima#Japan |deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: - [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection - [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): - [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – Deity of guidance and pathways - [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce - Ei Daijin (榮大神) – Deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * **Unryūsui (雲龍水)** – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * **Seiryūsui (青龍水)** – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)

[[ja:%E6%A4%8E%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:redirect]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[category:short pages]] [[category:short pages]][[Category:Shortest 100 pages as of 2025-02-13]] [[Category:new pages]] {{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city#Japan Tokushima#Japan |deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: - [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection - [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): - [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – Deity of guidance and pathways - [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce - Ei Daijin (榮大神) – Deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * **Unryūsui (雲龍水)** – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * **Seiryūsui (青龍水)** – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * **October 16** – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * **By Bus:** From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
- [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
- [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
- [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – Deity of guidance and pathways
- [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
- Ei Daijin (榮大神) – Deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

[[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]]
'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
- [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
- [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
- [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
- [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
- Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine}}

'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
- [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
- [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
- [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
- [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
- Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

[[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]]
'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
- [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
- [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
- [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
- [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
- Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
- [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
- [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
- [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
- [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
- Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
** [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
** [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
** [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)

</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.

[[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]]
'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
** [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

'''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main Deities:
** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection
** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi):
** [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways
** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce
** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) ==
The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including:
* Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社)
* Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity
* Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess
* Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area
* Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning
* Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History ==
The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here:
* '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps
* '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine
These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include:
- [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社)
- [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery>
File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park.
</gallery>
== Annual Events ==
* '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access ==
* '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles ==
* [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:椎宮八幡神社 up to rev 91243806
revision.text:
{{otheruses|徳島市佐古地区にある八幡神社|徳島市伊賀町にある八幡神社|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 椎宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[佐古山町]]椎宮1 | 緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =4 |緯度秒 =19.97 | 経度度 =134 |経度分 =31 |経度秒 =30.47 |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Tokushima city#Japan Tokushima#Japan |祭神 = [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]] |社格 = |創建 = 寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 椎宮神社 |例祭 = [[10月16日]] |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|つつじ祭りの風景。右は雲龍水。]] '''椎宮八幡神社'''（しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に鎮座する。通称「しいのみやさん」と呼ばれている。別名「'''椎宮神社'''」。椎宮八幡神社一帯は「'''椎宮公園'''」となっている。[[とくしま市民遺産]]選定。
== 祭神 == * [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]] * 合祀 - [[猿田彦神]]、[[神大市比売|大市姫命]]、榮大神
== 境内社 == * 中嶋神社、稲荷神社、神明社、鎮守社、天神社、疱瘡神社
== 歴史 == [[名東郡]]矢三村の古八幡宮（現・徳島市南矢三町１丁目10-26、{{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|位置|}}）より寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]）に当地へ遷座したと伝わる。古八幡宮には樹齢千年の楠木がある。当神社は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られ、約3,000本のツツジが植えられている。神社の東側斜面は全面をツツジで覆われており、毎年5月に満開を迎える。
境内には[[阿波国|阿波]]の民話「[[阿波狸合戦]]」に登場する狸を祀った祠がある他、正面石段の向かって左脇に[[眉山湧水群]]のひとつ雲龍水が湧き出し、神社裏には青龍水も湧き出している。これら湧水群は[[とくしま水紀行50選]]に選定されている。神社周辺には[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]や[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
<gallery> ファイル:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|椎宮公園から見た徳島市の景色。 </gallery>
== 祭事 == * [[10月16日]] - 例大祭
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古七番町]]」下車、徒歩約7分。
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html 椎宮神社のつつじ]（阿波ナビ）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しいのみやはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:眉山]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[椎宮八幡神社]] to [[Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{otheruses|徳島市佐古地区にある八幡神社|徳島市伊賀町にある八幡神社|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 椎宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[佐古山町]]椎宮1 | 緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =4 |緯度秒 =19.97 | 経度度 =134 |経度分 =31 |経度秒 =30.47 |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Tokushima city#Japan Tokushima#Japan |祭神 = [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]] |社格 = |創建 = 寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 椎宮神社 |例祭 = [[10月16日]] |神事 = }} [[ファイル:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|つつじ祭りの風景。右は雲龍水。]] '''椎宮八幡神社'''（しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に鎮座する。通称「しいのみやさん」と呼ばれている。別名「'''椎宮神社'''」。椎宮八幡神社一帯は「'''椎宮公園'''」となっている。[[とくしま市民遺産]]選定。
== 祭神 == * [[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫命]] * 合祀 - [[猿田彦神]]、[[神大市比売|大市姫命]]、榮大神
== 境内社 == * 中嶋神社、稲荷神社、神明社、鎮守社、天神社、疱瘡神社
== 歴史 == [[名東郡]]矢三村の古八幡宮（現・徳島市南矢三町１丁目10-26、{{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|位置|}}）より寛文年間（[[1661年]]-[[1673年]]）に当地へ遷座したと伝わる。古八幡宮には樹齢千年の楠木がある。当神社は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られ、約3,000本のツツジが植えられている。神社の東側斜面は全面をツツジで覆われており、毎年5月に満開を迎える。
境内には[[阿波国|阿波]]の民話「[[阿波狸合戦]]」に登場する狸を祀った祠がある他、正面石段の向かって左脇に[[眉山湧水群]]のひとつ雲龍水が湧き出し、神社裏には青龍水も湧き出している。これら湧水群は[[とくしま水紀行50選]]に選定されている。神社周辺には[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]や[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
<gallery> ファイル:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|椎宮公園から見た徳島市の景色。 </gallery>
== 祭事 == * [[10月16日]] - 例大祭
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古七番町]]」下車、徒歩約7分。
== 関連項目 == * [[八幡神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html 椎宮神社のつつじ]（阿波ナビ）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しいのみやはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:眉山]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)
{{shinto-stub}}

[[ja:%E6%A4%8E%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine]]
<!--Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine-->

{{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.

{{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg|300px]] {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=300}} |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)
{{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|椎宮八幡神社|version=91243806|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)
