* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11540834]] Wikidata name: Shiinomiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg |location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = |location = {{ill|Tokushima|qid=Q207309}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072214|N|134.525131|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11540834]] Wikidata name: Shiinomiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg |location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = |location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072214|N|134.525131|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)
{{shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11540834]] Wikidata name: Shiinomiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg |location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = |location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072214|N|134.525131|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11540834]] Wikidata name: Shiinomiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg |location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = |location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072214|N|134.525131|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001468.html Shinomiya Shrine Azaleas] (Awa Navi Tourism Guide)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11540834]] Wikidata name: Shiinomiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg |location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]] [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = |location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072214|N|134.525131|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mount Bizan]] and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan

* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity

Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
== Related Articles == * [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] (八幡神社) – General article about Hachiman shrines in Japan

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:椎宮八幡神社]]
{{otheruses|Hachiman Shrine located in Sako district, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine located in Iga Town, Tokushima City|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine |image = File:Shinomiya-Haciman-Jinja2.jpeg |location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima}} [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]] [[Sako-yamacho]] Shinomiya 1 |latitude_deg =34 |latitude_min =4 |latitude_sec =19.97 |longitude_deg =134 |longitude_min =31 |longitude_sec =30.47 |label_position = left |map = Japan Tokushima city=Japan Tokushima=Japan |deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (応神天皇), [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] (木花咲耶姫命) |shrine_Rank = |established = Kanbun era (1661–1673) |main_building = |alias = Shinomiya Shrine |annual_festival = [[October 16]] |rituals = |location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072214|N|134.525131|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} [[File:Un-Ryu-Sui.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Scene from the Azalea Festival. On the right is Unryūsui spring.]] '''Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine''' (しいのみやはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Mount Bizan|en|Mount Bizan|WD=Q8189109|lt=Mount Bizan}} and is locally known as "Shiinomiya-san" (しいのみやさん). It is also known by its alternative name, '''Shinomiya Shrine''' (椎宮神社). The area around the shrine is designated as '''Shinomiya Park''' (椎宮公園) and is listed as one of the [[Tokushima Civic Heritage Sites]] (とくしま市民遺産).
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities: ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (品陀別命) – A deity associated with Hachiman, the god of war and protection ** [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto]] (木花咲耶姫命) – A goddess of Mt. Fuji and blossoms, symbolizing life and beauty
* Additional Enshrined Deities (Gōshi): ** [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] (猿田彦神) – deity of guidance and pathways ** [[Kami-Ōichi-hime|Ōichihime-no-Mikoto]] (神大市比売) – Goddess of markets and commerce ** Ei Daijin (榮大神) – deity of prosperity
== Auxiliary Shrines (Keidai-sha) == The shrine grounds host several smaller shrines, including: * Nakajima Shrine (中嶋神社) * Inari Shrine (稲荷神社) – Dedicated to the deity of rice and prosperity * Shinmei Shrine (神明社) – Dedicated to Amaterasu, the sun goddess * Chinjusha Shrine (鎮守社) – A protective shrine for the local area * Tenjin Shrine (天神社) – Dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane, the deity of learning * Hōsō Shrine (疱瘡神社) – Dedicated to the deity of smallpox protection
== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.

== History == The shrine was relocated during the Kanbun era (1661–1673) from the old Hachiman Shrine of Yazo Village in Myodō District (現・徳島市南矢三町1丁目10-26, {{ウィキ座標|34|04|53.4|N|134|31|43.1|E|region:JP-36|label|}}). The original site, known as Furuhachimangū (古八幡宮), still stands with a sacred camphor tree over 1,000 years old.
Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine is renowned as a famous spot for azaleas (ツツジ). Approximately 3,000 azalea plants are planted around the eastern slopes of the shrine grounds. The blossoms reach their peak in May each year, covering the hillside in vibrant colors.
The shrine grounds are also significant for their natural springs. Two of the [[Mount Bizan Springs]] (眉山湧水群) can be found here: * '''Unryūsui (雲龍水)''' – Located to the left side of the front stone steps * '''Seiryūsui (青龍水)''' – Emerging from behind the shrine These springs are part of the [[Top 50 Tokushima Water Journeys]] (とくしま水紀行50選).
Additionally, a small shrine dedicated to the tanuki (raccoon dog) from the local Awa folklore, "[[Awa Tanuki Gassen]]" (阿波狸合戦), can be found within the grounds.
Nearby notable sites include: - [[Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|Suwa Shrine]] (諏訪神社) - [[Seisuiji Temple (Tokushima City)|Seisuiji Temple]] (清水寺)
<gallery> File:Shinomiya-Park.jpeg|View of Tokushima City from Shinomiya Park. </gallery>
== Annual Events == * '''October 16''' – Reitaisai (例大祭), the annual grand festival
== Access == * '''By Bus:''' From {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station|1=Tokushima Station}} (JR), take the [[Tokushima City Bus]] on the Kamiawui Line (上鮎喰線) and get off at "Sako 7-chome" (佐古七番町) stop. The shrine is a 7-minute walk from the stop.
